ground_truth.motive: Alina Rohe discovered that Bram Sayer had been padding festival reimbursements through repeated sham storage invoices and was about to confront him with a blue folder of proof.
ground_truth.means: Bram lured Alina to Gate C4 for a private confrontation, killed her there off-route, then moved her body beneath the North Arch and let a partial arch failure reinforce the appearance of an accident.
ground_truth.opportunity: Bram checked out the service master key and reassigned the only nearby marshal, creating a private five-minute window at the old gate.

timeline.event: Bram checks out service master key SVC-MASTER-1.
Bram reroutes Post 3 away from Gate C4.
Alina leaves the review table carrying the blue folder.
Alina is reported beneath the North Arch.
Bram returns the service master key.

red_herrings: Ivo Pell worked on the North Arch earlier that evening and appears capable of causing a hardware accident.
Sera Holt knew about the suspicious reimbursements and could be mistaken for someone with financial motive.
Mara Venn was assigned closest to Gate C4 before being rerouted, which makes her seem like a witness withholding something.

characters.bio: Route director for the Emberwake procession. Organized, respected, and quietly building a finance discrepancy file the week she died.
Deputy route director responsible for staffing changes and service access during final setup.

alibis.claim: He was solving sponsor-entry congestion during the critical window.

characters.bio: Float carpenter who signed off on North Arch hardware and becomes the obvious accident-based suspect.

alibis.claim: He remained at the North Arch during final adjustments.

characters.bio: Festival treasurer who worked with Alina on reimbursement approvals and later confirms what the missing folder contained.

alibis.claim: She remained at the sponsor desk during final review.

characters.bio: Marshal originally stationed at Gate C4 who lost sight of the lane after a last-minute reroute.

alibis.claim: She was moved off the service lane before Alina arrived there.

objectives.prompt: Which gate code matches the meeting point in the victim's note?

hint_ladder.hint: The note gives a nickname, not a code. Translate it using the map.
Only one location on the map is labeled as an older gate.
Find the marker labeled 'Old Service Gate.'

hint_ladder.reveal: The meeting point is Gate C4.

objectives.prompt: Which two findings prove someone deliberately created a private meeting window at Gate C4?

hint_ladder.hint: One clue controls the lock. Another removes the witness.
Check who held the service master key, then check what happened to Post 3.
Bram's name appears in both the key log and the staffing change.

hint_ladder.reveal: The private window is proved by Bram checking out SVC-MASTER-1 and rerouting Post 3 away from Gate C4.

objectives.prompt: Who killed Alina Rohe and staged the North Arch accident?

hint_ladder.hint: Follow motive now. Who feared the blue folder most?
The treasurer names the person Alina intended to confront.
The search report puts a trace of the missing folder in the same suspect's cart.

hint_ladder.reveal: Bram Sayer killed Alina Rohe and staged the body beneath the North Arch.

content.text: Ashfall intake summary of Alina Rohe's apparent festival-setup death and initial suspect pool.

item_hints.text: The brief tells you what matters first: not the killer, but the victim's intended destination.
The first stage is about locating the meeting point, not solving the whole murder.

content.text: Official account placing Alina beneath the North Arch and noting the missing blue folder.

item_hints.text: Treat the report as the official version, not the trustworthy version.
The report matters most because it records the missing blue folder and the note recovered from Alina's coat.

content.text: Map of the route and service lane showing Old Service Gate C4.

item_hints.text: One label on the map translates the note's vague wording into a code.
The old gate mentioned in the note is Gate C4.

content.text: Unsigned note directing Alina to the old gate before first bell with the blue folder.

item_hints.text: This note points away from the discovery scene.
The note sets up a meeting at the old gate and explains why Alina left with the folder.

content.text: Checkpoint entries showing the route was normal until Alina left the review table.

item_hints.text: Use the checkpoint log to anchor when Alina stopped being publicly visible.
The log shows Alina left the review table with the blue folder at 18:46 and did not return.

content.text: Checkout record for service access keys, including Bram's use of SVC-MASTER-1.

item_hints.text: Ask who could physically open the gate you identified.
Bram checked out the only service master key before the meeting and returned it after the incident.

content.text: Last-minute staffing changes issued by Bram minutes before first bell.

item_hints.text: It is not enough to open the gate. Someone also needed the lane unwatched.
Bram moved Post 3 away from Gate C4, creating a private window.

content.transcript: Sera Holt confirms that Alina planned to confront Bram over duplicate storage reimbursements and that Bram promised a private explanation at the old gate.

item_hints.text: This interview tells you why the meeting happened at all.
Sera says Alina planned to confront Bram and that Bram requested the private meeting at the old gate.

content.text: Duplicate storage reimbursements repeatedly approved by Bram and flagged by Alina.

item_hints.text: Look for a repeated pattern, not a single large number.
The ledger shows Bram approved repeated sham storage payments that Alina flagged for review.

content.text: Search report describing blue-folder hardware and a C4-marked route copy recovered from Bram's cart.

item_hints.text: This is the closest thing to the missing folder itself.
A broken clasp matching the blue finance folders was recovered from Bram's cart along with a route copy marked at C4.
